description: Local educational SOC response playbooks. Synthetic data only; no real incident response is performed.

playbooks.title: SSH Brute Force Response
steps: Confirm whether the source IP is expected.
Check account lockout and MFA status for the targeted users.
Review whether any successful login followed the failed attempts.
Temporarily block the source IP if activity is unauthorized.
containment: Block the source IP at the perimeter firewall.
Force password reset for any targeted accounts with weak passwords.
evidence_to_collect: Authentication logs (sshd) for the relevant window.
Successful login records for the targeted accounts.
Source IP reputation data from internal threat-intel notes.
playbooks.limitations: This is a local educational playbook using fake data.

playbooks.title: Web Application Attack Response
steps: Identify the targeted endpoints and HTTP methods.
Correlate alerts by source IP and user-agent.
Confirm whether the application returned sensitive data.
Engage application owners for impact assessment.
containment: Apply WAF signatures for the observed payloads.
Rate-limit or block the source IP at the edge.
Disable the targeted endpoint if it is unintentionally exposed.
evidence_to_collect: Web access logs for the entire campaign window.
Application server error logs.
WAF telemetry and any DB-side audit logs.
playbooks.limitations: This is a local educational playbook using fake data.

playbooks.title: SQL Injection Response
steps: Verify the injection payload reached the database.
Check for data extraction in DB-side query logs.
Identify the application code path that allowed the injection.
containment: Apply WAF signatures or input validation.
Virtual-patch the vulnerable endpoint.
Rotate any credentials that may have been exposed.
evidence_to_collect: Full web request bodies for the campaign.
Database query logs.
Application stack traces.
playbooks.limitations: This is a local educational playbook using fake data.

playbooks.title: Cross-Site Scripting Response
steps: Identify the injected payload and the reflection point.
Check whether the payload is stored, reflected, or DOM-based.
Identify affected users (cookies, session state).
containment: Apply output-encoding fix on the vulnerable endpoint.
Invalidate affected user sessions.
Add CSP header to limit script execution.
evidence_to_collect: Request and response bodies for the affected endpoint.
Session activity for any user that loaded the injected page.
playbooks.limitations: This is a local educational playbook using fake data.

playbooks.title: Directory Traversal Response
steps: Confirm whether sensitive files were served.
Identify the vulnerable file-serving code path.
Determine whether file integrity has changed.
containment: Apply path canonicalization in the application.
Restrict the application user's filesystem access.
Block the source IP at the edge if necessary.
evidence_to_collect: Web access logs for the traversal window.
Application server file-access audit logs.
playbooks.limitations: This is a local educational playbook using fake data.

playbooks.title: DNS Beacon / C2 Response
steps: Identify the originating host and any peers.
Determine the cadence and stability of the beacon.
Inspect the suspect host for known persistence mechanisms.
containment: Sinkhole the suspicious domain(s).
Isolate the originating host from the network.
Block outbound DNS to non-corporate resolvers.
evidence_to_collect: Full DNS query history for the affected host.
Endpoint process telemetry for the same window.
Any associated outbound HTTP/HTTPS connection logs.
playbooks.limitations: This is a local educational playbook using fake data.

playbooks.title: Port Scan / Network Recon Response
steps: Identify the scanning source and the destination targets.
Confirm whether any ports were actually open.
Check for follow-on connection attempts beyond the scan.
containment: Block the scanning source at the perimeter.
Confirm exposed services are intentional.
Increase IDS sensitivity for the targeted subnet.
evidence_to_collect: Firewall logs for the scanning window.
Any IDS / NetFlow telemetry.
playbooks.limitations: This is a local educational playbook using fake data.

playbooks.title: Privilege Escalation Response
steps: Audit sudo / privileged commands for the named user.
Verify the integrity of /etc/shadow and /etc/sudoers.
Check process and command-line telemetry for the same window.
containment: Disable the affected user account temporarily.
Rotate root and service credentials.
Restore /etc/shadow and /etc/sudoers from a clean backup if modified.
evidence_to_collect: Auth logs and sudo logs.
Filesystem audit logs (auditd) for /etc/shadow and /etc/sudoers.
Endpoint process telemetry.
playbooks.limitations: This is a local educational playbook using fake data.
